combined_verdict: REFUTED across all tested persistent-regime pairs -- period-level config-selection does NOT transfer even when the regime persists. The prior null was a REAL LIMIT, not just the crash confound. High-value honest result.

pairs.label: SUSTAINED BULL 2024-02->03
pairs.probe: u10 M1 +29.2%/100%-up, M2 +22.5%/80%-up; worst-asset-DD M2 -29% (normal vol, no market-wide shock -- book stayed +22.5%); regime persist 6/10

2_sub_period.CAVEAT: weekly sub-windows OVERLAP MA warmup + are autocorrelated -> supporting evidence only, NOT independent samples

3_block_bootstrap.note: resample month-1 per-config trade returns in blocks -> rank by median/p25

4_synthetic.note: score each config across synthetic Feb-like paths -> prefer robust

5_shrinkage.note: B near 0 => heavy shrink (config differences are noise vs the bootstrap noise floor); B near 1 => differences clear the noise = signal

6_regime_to_config.honest_note: map COLLAPSES to ONE config for all buckets -> the edge (if any) is a SINGLE-AXIS dominance (e.g. slow-MA-trades-rarely-survives-cost), NOT learned regime navigation. Report as such.

config_rank_persistence.interpretation: rho~+1: config ranking PERSISTS month1->month2 (regime->config transfers); rho~0: last month's config ranking is uninformative for next month (thesis FAILS even in the fair case); rho<0: anti-persistent (winner becomes loser)

verdict.headline: REFUTED (FAIR CASE): even with the regime PERSISTING month1->month2, the map COLLAPSES to one config on a majority (0/4 differentiate) -- the cost-avoidance signature, NOT learned regime navigation; it stays FAR from the oracle (>50% capture on only 0/4) -- beating random is not the same as approaching the hindsight best. So the prior NULL was NOT merely the crash confound -- period-level config-selection does NOT transfer even in the fair case. The honest result: 'config-selection doesn't transfer even when the regime persists.' Reported as a refutation, not spun.
lines: PERSISTENT-REGIME FAIR TEST -- SUSTAINED BULL 2024-02->03.
Regime persistence MEASURED: 6/10 assets keep the same regime month1->month2 (0.6); train mix {'up': 10} -> test mix {'up': 6, 'flat': 4}.

HEADLINE: REFUTED (FAIR CASE): even with the regime PERSISTING month1->month2, the map COLLAPSES to one config on a majority (0/4 differentiate) -- the cost-avoidance signature, NOT learned regime navigation; it stays FAR from the oracle (>50% capture on only 0/4) -- beating random is not the same as approaching the hindsight best. So the prior NULL was NOT merely the crash confound -- period-level config-selection does NOT transfer even in the fair case. The honest result: 'config-selection doesn't transfer even when the regime persists.' Reported as a refutation, not spun.
[4h] config-rank rho(book)=0.403 | map-differentiates=False (1 cfgs) | pred 5.97% / oracle 21.21% (ratio 0.28) | book BEATS random
[1h] config-rank rho(book)=0.672 | map-differentiates=False (1 cfgs) | pred 4.03% / oracle 17.27% (ratio 0.23) | book BEATS random
[30m] config-rank rho(book)=0.819 | map-differentiates=False (1 cfgs) | pred 4.66% / oracle 19.8% (ratio 0.24) | book BEATS random
[15m] config-rank rho(book)=0.869 | map-differentiates=False (1 cfgs) | pred 4.78% / oracle 17.18% (ratio 0.28) | book BEATS random
Q1 map differentiates on 0/4 cadences (majority=False).
Q2 approaches oracle (>50% capture) on 0/4 (majority=False).
Q4 config-rank persists (book rho>+0.30) on 4/4 (majority=True); mean book rho=0.691.

pairs.label: CALM RANGE->mild-up 2023-09->10
pairs.probe: u10 M1 +5.4%/70%-up (low vol), M2 +18.4%/100%-up; worst-asset-DD ~-16%; regime persist 6/10 -- a CALMER, different regime for robustness

2_sub_period.CAVEAT: weekly sub-windows OVERLAP MA warmup + are autocorrelated -> supporting evidence only, NOT independent samples

verdict.headline: REFUTED (FAIR CASE): even with the regime PERSISTING month1->month2, it stays FAR from the oracle (>50% capture on only 1/4) -- beating random is not the same as approaching the hindsight best. So the prior NULL was NOT merely the crash confound -- period-level config-selection does NOT transfer even in the fair case. The honest result: 'config-selection doesn't transfer even when the regime persists.' Reported as a refutation, not spun.
lines: PERSISTENT-REGIME FAIR TEST -- CALM RANGE->mild-up 2023-09->10.
Regime persistence MEASURED: 6/10 assets keep the same regime month1->month2 (0.6); train mix {'up': 3, 'flat': 6, 'down': 1} -> test mix {'up': 7, 'flat': 3}.

HEADLINE: REFUTED (FAIR CASE): even with the regime PERSISTING month1->month2, it stays FAR from the oracle (>50% capture on only 1/4) -- beating random is not the same as approaching the hindsight best. So the prior NULL was NOT merely the crash confound -- period-level config-selection does NOT transfer even in the fair case. The honest result: 'config-selection doesn't transfer even when the regime persists.' Reported as a refutation, not spun.
[4h] config-rank rho(book)=0.506 | map-differentiates=True (2 cfgs) | pred 4.83% / oracle 24.0% (ratio 0.2) | book LOSES random
[1h] config-rank rho(book)=0.634 | map-differentiates=True (2 cfgs) | pred 3.55% / oracle 23.84% (ratio 0.15) | book LOSES random
[30m] config-rank rho(book)=0.842 | map-differentiates=True (4 cfgs) | pred 10.66% / oracle 24.19% (ratio 0.44) | book BEATS random
[15m] config-rank rho(book)=0.912 | map-differentiates=True (2 cfgs) | pred 17.81% / oracle 22.5% (ratio 0.79) | book BEATS random
Q1 map differentiates on 4/4 cadences (majority=True).
Q2 approaches oracle (>50% capture) on 1/4 (majority=False).
Q4 config-rank persists (book rho>+0.30) on 4/4 (majority=True); mean book rho=0.724.
